data.title: ASML Lifts 2026 Outlook As AI Demand Grows And Metrics Shift
data.description:
• ASML Holding raised its full year 2026 sales forecast after reporting strong Q1 results, citing faster demand for its chip making tools tied to AI.
  • The company said AI driven semiconductor investments and robust customer orders are supporting its higher sales expectations.
  • ASML will stop disclosing quarterly bookings, shifting investor attention toward reported sales and management commentary.

For investors watching ENXTAM:ASML, this guidance change lands on top of a share price of €1,284.2 and very strong multi year returns, including 30.2% year to date and 114.0% over the past 12 months. Those moves reflect how central ASML’s tools have become to advanced chip production as AI related spending grows across data centers and high performance computing.

The higher 2026 sales outlook and new disclosure approach could change how you track ASML’s progress, with less emphasis on quarterly order swings and more on actual revenue trends and management signals. As markets digest this shift, investors may pay closer attention to how AI related capacity plans at ASML’s major customers translate into equipment demand over the next few years.

data.title: S&P Global Expands AI Energy Insights While Mobility Tracks Used Car Volatility
data.description:
• S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) has completed the acquisition of Enertel AI Corporation.
  • The deal adds AI driven short term power price forecasting to S&P Global's energy division.
  • The move comes as its Mobility unit continues to track volatility in the used car market.

S&P Global, known for its credit ratings, benchmarks, and data intelligence, is leaning further into energy analytics with the Enertel AI acquisition. By folding Enertel's forecasting tools into its energy division, the company is adding a new layer of short term pricing insight that can matter for utilities, traders, and large power users. At the same time, its Mobility unit's coverage of used car price volatility is keeping attention on how real time data can influence decisions in other markets.

For you as an investor, these moves highlight how S&P Global is tying more of its offering to transactional and price data across different sectors. Energy clients looking for AI enabled tools and auto market participants watching used car price swings may both find more reasons to rely on the company's platforms. This may influence how you think about the breadth and resilience of NYSE:SPGI's information ecosystem.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for S&P Global by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on S&P Global.

We've flagged 0 risks for S&P Global. See which could impact your investment.

The Enertel AI deal pushes S&P Global further into real time, transaction-adjacent data that can sit alongside its traditional ratings and index franchises. For you, the key angle is how AI-powered short term power price forecasts and Mobility’s used car pricing data both lean on the same core strengths: detailed datasets and analytics that customers can plug directly into day to day decisions. In energy, utilities, traders and large corporates often need highly granular, short horizon signals to manage risk and contract exposure. That sits close to what peers such as MSCI, Moody’s and Bloomberg are building around climate, commodities and transportation data. If S&P Global can cross sell Enertel’s tools into its existing energy client base and align them with Mobility insights, the company could deepen integration into customer workflows rather than just adding another standalone product.

How This Fits Into The S&P Global Narrative

  • Adding AI-powered power price forecasting supports the broader story of S&P Global leaning on data and analytics capabilities to extend into adjacent use cases such as energy trading and utilities risk management.
  • Heavier focus on AI tools could challenge the narrative if development and integration costs rise faster than client adoption or pricing power in the energy division.
  • The link between Enertel’s short term power data and the Mobility unit’s used car pricing coverage may not be fully captured yet, particularly any benefits from sharing infrastructure or creating cross sector datasets.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for S&P Global to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Integrating Enertel’s technology, people and datasets into S&P Global’s energy division could take longer or cost more than expected, which may weigh on margins for a period.
  • ⚠️ Energy and power markets are crowded with data providers, including large firms such as Bloomberg and ICE, so there is a risk that pricing pressure or client churn limits the commercial impact of this acquisition.
  • 🎁 If S&P Global successfully embeds Enertel’s forecasts into existing platforms, it could deepen client reliance and support cross selling to utilities, traders and large corporates already using other S&P Global services.
  • 🎁 The combination of AI-driven power price data and Mobility’s used car analytics gives S&P Global more ways to serve customers who care about both energy costs and transportation trends, which may widen the addressable customer base.

What To Watch Going Forward

Over the coming quarters, keep an eye on how frequently management references Enertel in product updates for the energy division, and whether new customer wins or use cases are tied directly to AI-powered forecasting. It is also worth tracking how S&P Global talks about capital allocation between Ratings, data platforms and AI investments, especially as the company prepares to report Q1 2026 results and commentators focus on earnings quality and margins. Any disclosure on adoption rates, contract sizes or bundled offerings that include both energy and Mobility datasets can help you judge how effectively this acquisition is being turned into recurring, workflow-embedded revenue rather than remaining a niche add on.

data.description:

Over recent periods, quarterly revenue has ranged from ¥36.4b in FY 2025 Q4 to ¥40.4b in FY 2026 Q2, while quarterly EPS has moved between ¥24.95 and ¥47.83. This gives investors a clear view of how top line scale and per share profitability have tracked together as margins held relatively steady.

data.title: Eco'sLtd (TSE:7520) Net Margin Squeeze To 1.9% Tests Bullish Valuation Narrative
data.description:
Eco'sLtd (TSE:7520) has just wrapped up FY 2026 with Q4 revenue of ¥33.7b and a basic EPS loss of ¥56.61, capping off a year where trailing twelve month revenue stood at ¥138.0b and EPS reached ¥235.53. Over the past six reported quarters, the company has seen quarterly revenue move in a tight band between ¥33.5b and ¥35.6b, while basic EPS has ranged from a Q4 loss of ¥56.61 to a high of ¥132.20. Trailing twelve month EPS has fluctuated between ¥324.45 and ¥400.97. For investors, this mix of steady top line and more uneven EPS invites a closer look at how margins are holding up through one off hits and softer profitability.

See our full analysis for Eco'sLtd.

With the latest numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up against the key stories investors have been following about Eco'sLtd and where those narratives might need updating.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Margins Thin at 1.9% Net Level

  • Over the last 12 months, Eco'sLtd converted ¥137,984 million of revenue into ¥2,645 million of net income, which works out to a 1.9% net margin compared with 3% the prior year.
  • Critics highlight a bearish concern that thinner margins make the business more fragile, and the data backs some of that, with net margin moving from 3% to 1.9%, while quarterly net income over FY 2026 ranged from a ¥1,485 million profit in Q2 to a ¥636 million loss in Q4.
    • This spread in quarterly net income, from profit to loss, shows how quickly profitability can swing even though revenue stayed in a tight band between ¥33,548 million and ¥35,601 million across the last six reported quarters.
    • That pattern is consistent with a bearish view that small changes in costs or pricing can have a meaningful effect on the bottom line when the margin starts from a low base.

¥2.0b One Off Loss Distorts Trailing EPS

  • Trailing twelve month EPS stepped down from ¥400.97 at FY 2026 Q3 to ¥235.53 at FY 2026 Q4, in a period that included a ¥2.0b one off loss that materially reduced reported earnings.
  • What is surprising for a bearish narrative that focuses on weak current earnings is that this large one off charge sits alongside five year earnings growth that averaged 13.5% per year, so the trailing 12 month EPS of ¥235.53 reflects both that growth record and the impact of the ¥2.0b item.
    • Bears may focus on the most recent year showing negative earnings growth versus that 13.5% multi year average, but the data separates a structural margin level of 1.9% from a single, identified loss.
    • Investors weighing this cautious view can see that Q4 alone showed a basic EPS loss of ¥56.61 and net income of ¥636 million in the red, which sharpens the question of how recurring that pattern might be without the one off.

Low 10x P/E Versus 42.6x Peers

  • The shares trade at a trailing P/E of 10x, compared with a peer average of 42.6x and a JP market average of 14.7x, while the current share price of ¥2,345 sits below the DCF fair value of ¥2,004.26 that some investors may track.
  • Supporters of a more bullish angle on valuation point out that this lower multiple sits alongside five year earnings growth of 13.5% per year, yet the same dataset flags that net margin slid to 1.9% and the 2.99% dividend yield is not well covered by free cash flow.
    • This mix heavily supports the bullish case that the stock looks inexpensive on a simple earnings multiple, but it also shows why some investors pay close attention to how quickly margins and free cash flow coverage might change.
    • On the other side, the weak cash flow support for the dividend and the recent negative earnings year give cautious investors specific numbers to track rather than treating the 10x P/E as an all clear signal.

data.title: Analysts Just Published A Bright New Outlook For BW Energy Limited's (OB:BWE)
data.description:
BW Energy Limited (OB:BWE) shareholders will have a reason to smile today, with the analysts making substantial upgrades to this year's forecasts. Consensus estimates suggest investors could expect greatly increased statutory revenues and earnings per share, with analysts modelling a real improvement in business performance.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Following the upgrade, the current consensus from BW Energy's three analysts is for revenues of US$890m in 2026 which - if met - would reflect a solid 14% increase on its sales over the past 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to soar 31% to US$0.67. Previously, the analysts had been modelling revenues of US$799m and earnings per share (EPS) of US$0.38 in 2026. There has definitely been an improvement in perception recently, with the analysts substantially increasing both their earnings and revenue estimates.

Check out our latest analysis for BW Energy

Taking a look at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can understand these forecasts is to see how they compare to both past performance and industry growth estimates. It's pretty clear that there is an expectation that BW Energy's revenue growth will slow down substantially, with revenues to the end of 2026 expected to display 14% growth on an annualised basis. This is compared to a historical growth rate of 33% over the past five years. Compare this with other companies in the same industry, which are forecast to see a revenue decline of 1.4% annually. Factoring in the forecast slowdown in growth, it's pretty clear that BW Energy is still expected to grow faster than the wider industry.

The biggest takeaway for us from these new estimates is that analysts upgraded their earnings per share estimates, with improved earnings power expected for this year. data.title: Be Sure To Check Out Ferrari N.V. (BIT:RACE) Before It Goes Ex-Dividend
data.description:
Ferrari N.V. (BIT:RACE) is about to trade ex-dividend in the next four days. The ex-dividend date is two business days before a company's record date in most cases, which is the date on which the company determines which shareholders are entitled to receive a dividend. It is important to be aware of the ex-dividend date because any trade on the stock needs to have been settled on or before the record date. This means that investors who purchase Ferrari's shares on or after the 20th of April will not receive the dividend, which will be paid on the 5th of May.

The company's upcoming dividend is €3.615 a share, following on from the last 12 months, when the company distributed a total of €3.62 per share to shareholders. Based on the last year's worth of payments, Ferrari stock has a trailing yield of around 1.2% on the current share price of €305.35. Dividends are a major contributor to investment returns for long term holders, but only if the dividend continues to be paid. As a result, readers should always check whether Ferrari has been able to grow its dividends, or if the dividend might be cut.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

If a company pays out more in dividends than it earned, then the dividend might become unsustainable - hardly an ideal situation. Ferrari paid out a comfortable 40% of its profit last year. Yet cash flows are even more important than profits for assessing a dividend, so we need to see if the company generated enough cash to pay its distribution. Thankfully its dividend payments took up just 38% of the free cash flow it generated, which is a comfortable payout ratio.

It's positive to see that Ferrari's dividend is covered by both profits and cash flow, since this is generally a sign that the dividend is sustainable, and a lower payout ratio usually suggests a greater margin of safety before the dividend gets cut.

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Businesses with strong growth prospects usually make the best dividend payers, because it's easier to grow dividends when earnings per share are improving. Investors love dividends, so if earnings fall and the dividend is reduced, expect a stock to be sold off heavily at the same time. That's why it's comforting to see Ferrari's earnings have been skyrocketing, up 22% per annum for the past five years. Earnings per share have been growing very quickly, and the company is paying out a relatively low percentage of its profit and cash flow. This is a very favourable combination that can often lead to the dividend multiplying over the long term, if earnings grow and the company pays out a higher percentage of its earnings.

Many investors will assess a company's dividend performance by evaluating how much the dividend payments have changed over time. In the past 10 years, Ferrari has increased its dividend at approximately 23% a year on average. It's exciting to see that both earnings and dividends per share have grown rapidly over the past few years.

To Sum It Up

From a dividend perspective, should investors buy or avoid Ferrari? Ferrari has grown its earnings per share while simultaneously reinvesting in the business. Unfortunately it's cut the dividend at least once in the past 10 years, but the conservative payout ratio makes the current dividend look sustainable. There's a lot to like about Ferrari, and we would prioritise taking a closer look at it.

data.title: We Don’t Think ASAS Makeen Real Estate Development and Investment's (TADAWUL:9640) Earnings Should Make Shareholders Too Comfortable
data.description:
ASAS Makeen Real Estate Development and Investment Company (TADAWUL:9640) posted some decent earnings, but shareholders didn't react strongly. We think that they might be concerned about some underlying details that our analysis found.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Examining Cashflow Against ASAS Makeen Real Estate Development and Investment's Earnings

As finance nerds would already know, the accrual ratio from cashflow is a key measure for assessing how well a company's free cash flow (FCF) matches its profit. To get the accrual ratio we first subtract FCF from profit for a period, and then divide that number by the average operating assets for the period. This ratio tells us how much of a company's profit is not backed by free cashflow.

That means a negative accrual ratio is a good thing, because it shows that the company is bringing in more free cash flow than its profit would suggest. While it's not a problem to have a positive accrual ratio, indicating a certain level of non-cash profits, a high accrual ratio is arguably a bad thing, because it indicates paper profits are not matched by cash flow. That's because some academic studies have suggested that high accruals ratios tend to lead to lower profit or less profit growth.

For the year to December 2025, ASAS Makeen Real Estate Development and Investment had an accrual ratio of 0.52. Statistically speaking, that's a real negative for future earnings. To wit, the company did not generate one whit of free cashflow in that time. Over the last year it actually had negative free cash flow of ر.س238m, in contrast to the aforementioned profit of ر.س129.9m. Coming off the back of negative free cash flow last year, we imagine some shareholders might wonder if its cash burn of ر.س238m, this year, indicates high risk. Having said that, there is more to the story. We can see that unusual items have impacted its statutory profit, and therefore the accrual ratio.

View our latest analysis for ASAS Makeen Real Estate Development and Investment

Note: we always recommend investors check balance sheet strength. Click here to be taken to our balance sheet analysis of ASAS Makeen Real Estate Development and Investment.

The Impact Of Unusual Items On Profit

The fact that the company had unusual items boosting profit by ر.س74m, in the last year, probably goes some way to explain why its accrual ratio was so weak. While we like to see profit increases, we tend to be a little more cautious when unusual items have made a big contribution. When we crunched the numbers on thousands of publicly listed companies, we found that a boost from unusual items in a given year is often not repeated the next year. And, after all, that's exactly what the accounting terminology implies. We can see that ASAS Makeen Real Estate Development and Investment's positive unusual items were quite significant relative to its profit in the year to December 2025. All else being equal, this would likely have the effect of making the statutory profit a poor guide to underlying earnings power.

Our Take On ASAS Makeen Real Estate Development and Investment's Profit Performance

Summing up, ASAS Makeen Real Estate Development and Investment received a nice boost to profit from unusual items, but could not match its paper profit with free cash flow. For all the reasons mentioned above, we think that, at a glance, ASAS Makeen Real Estate Development and Investment's statutory profits could be considered to be low quality, because they are likely to give investors an overly positive impression of the company. With this in mind, we wouldn't consider investing in a stock unless we had a thorough understanding of the risks.
